2603.30040.title: Automatic Identification of Parallelizable Loops Using Transformer-Based Source Code Representations
2603.30040.paper_summary: Automatic parallelization remains a challenging problem in software engineering, particularly in identifying code regions where loops can be safely executed in parallel on modern multi-core architectures. Traditional static analysis techniques, such as dependence analysis and polyhedral models, often struggle with irregular or dynamically structured code. In this work, we propose a Transformer-based approach to classify the parallelization potential of source code, focusing on distinguishing independent (parallelizable) loops from undefined ones. We adopt DistilBERT to process source code sequences using subword tokenization, enabling the model to capture contextual syntactic and semantic patterns without handcrafted features. The approach is evaluated on a balanced dataset combining synthetically generated loops and manually annotated real-world code, using 10-fold cross-validation and multiple performance metrics. Results show consistently high performance, with mean accuracy above 99\% and low false positive rates, demonstrating robustness and reliability. Compared to prior token-based methods, the proposed approach simplifies preprocessing while improving generalization and maintaining computational efficiency. These findings highlight the potential of lightweight Transformer models for practical identification of parallelization opportunities at the loop level.
2603.30040.paper_summary_zh: 自動平行化仍然是軟體工程中的一個挑戰性問題，特別是在識別可以安全地在現代多核心架構上平行執行的迴圈代碼區域方面。傳統的靜態分析技術，如依賴分析和多面體模型，往往在處理不規則或動態結構的代碼時遇到困難。在本研究中，我們提出了一種基於Transformer的方法來分類源代碼的平行化潛力，重點區分獨立（可平行化）迴圈與未定義的迴圈。我們採用DistilBERT來處理源代碼序列，使用子詞標記化，使模型能夠捕捉上下文語法和語義模式，而無需手工特徵。該方法在一個平衡的數據集上進行評估，該數據集結合了合成生成的迴圈和手動標註的現實世界代碼，使用10倍交叉驗證和多個性能指標。結果顯示出穩定的高性能，平均準確率超過99\%且假陽性率低，顯示出穩健性和可靠性。與先前的基於標記的方法相比，所提出的方法簡化了預處理，同時提高了泛化能力並保持計算效率。這些發現突顯了輕量級Transformer模型在實際識別迴圈級平行化機會方面的潛力。
2603.30040.author: Izavan dos S. Correia et.al.
2603.30040.authors: Izavan dos S. Correia, Henrique C. T. Santos, Tiago A. E. Ferreira
